description: Post-v6 code review fixes: security vulnerabilities, 300-line limit violations, broken tests, null-check crashes, missing user_id in DB queries, and extension hardening. Followed by new features: real-time notifications, custom fields, document uploads, and deadline tracking.

userStories.title: Security: add user_id to _score_and_update DB query
userStories.description: The _score_and_update function in applications/service.py updates ai_analysis using only _id in the MongoDB filter, without user_id. This is a privilege escalation bug — any background task could overwrite another user's application if app_id collides or is manipulated. Add user_id to the update_one filter.
acceptanceCriteria: applications/service.py _score_and_update update_one filter includes both {'_id': ObjectId(app_id), 'user_id': user_id}
If the update matches 0 documents (wrong user), log a warning via structlog and return without error
Add a test in test_applications.py: test_score_update_rejects_wrong_user — create app as user A, call _score_and_update with user B's id, assert ai_analysis is NOT set
All existing tests pass
userStories.notes: SECURITY FIX. This is the highest priority item.

userStories.title: Fix test_auth_google.py mock path to use oauth_service
userStories.description: test_auth_google.py patches 'auth.service.httpx.AsyncClient' but google_verify_id_token lives in auth/oauth_service.py where httpx is imported. The mock target should be 'auth.oauth_service.httpx.AsyncClient'. This causes test_google_verify_id_token_returns_claims_on_200 to fail with 'AttributeError: module auth.service has no attribute httpx'.
acceptanceCriteria: All patch() calls in test_auth_google.py use 'auth.oauth_service.httpx.AsyncClient' instead of 'auth.service.httpx.AsyncClient'
All tests in test_auth_google.py pass: run pytest tests/test_auth_google.py -v and confirm 0 failures
No other test files are modified
Full test suite passes: pytest tests/ -x shows 0 failures
userStories.notes: Simple find-and-replace fix across the file.

userStories.title: Split applications/service.py into focused modules under 300 lines
userStories.description: applications/service.py is 1346 lines — over 4x the 300-line limit. Split it into: service.py (CRUD core), service_export.py (CSV export, PDF report), service_analytics.py (stats, funnel, stage metrics), service_ai.py (fit scoring, AI analysis), service_bulk.py (bulk update/delete). Each file must be under 300 lines. Update all imports in router.py and tests.
acceptanceCriteria: applications/service.py is under 300 lines and contains only CRUD operations (create, get, update, delete, list)
applications/service_export.py contains export_applications_csv and generate_pdf_report functions
applications/service_analytics.py contains compute_stats, get_funnel, and stage metric functions
applications/service_ai.py contains _score_and_update, score_fit integration, and AI-related helpers
applications/service_bulk.py contains bulk_update and bulk_delete functions
Every new file is under 300 lines
applications/router.py imports are updated to use the new module paths
All tests pass: pytest tests/ -x shows 0 failures
No function exceeds 40 lines — split compute_stats and get_funnel if needed
userStories.notes: Use relative imports within the applications package. Keep shared constants (LIST_PROJECTION, STAGE_ORDER) in a service_constants.py if needed.

userStories.title: Split applications/router.py to stay under 300 lines
userStories.description: applications/router.py is 399 lines. Extract bulk operations and analytics/export endpoints into router_bulk.py and router_analytics.py. Use FastAPI APIRouter with prefix and include_router in the main router.
acceptanceCriteria: applications/router.py is under 300 lines and contains only core CRUD endpoints (list, get, create, update, delete)
applications/router_bulk.py contains the POST /bulk-update and related bulk endpoints
applications/router_analytics.py contains GET /stats, GET /funnel, GET /export, GET /report endpoints
Both new files use APIRouter and are included via router.include_router() in the main router.py
Every new file is under 300 lines
All existing tests pass: pytest tests/ -x shows 0 failures
API contract unchanged: all endpoints respond at the same paths with same status codes
userStories.notes: The main router.py should include_router the sub-routers at the top.

userStories.title: Split oversized frontend files: LandingPage, DetailPanel, ApplicationList, PrepSection
userStories.description: Four frontend files exceed 300 lines: LandingPage.jsx (323), DetailPanel.jsx (312), ApplicationList.jsx (309), PrepSection.jsx (304). Extract sub-components to bring each under the limit.
acceptanceCriteria: LandingPage.jsx under 300 lines: extract HeroSection.jsx, FeatureShowcase.jsx, or SocialProof.jsx as needed
DetailPanel.jsx under 300 lines: extract DetailPanelActions.jsx or DetailPanelStage.jsx for stage/action logic
ApplicationList.jsx under 300 lines: extract ApplicationListHeader.jsx or BulkActionBar refinements
PrepSection.jsx under 300 lines: extract PrepChecklist.jsx or PrepQuestions.jsx sub-components
Each extracted component has at least one smoke test (renders without crash)
All existing tests pass: npm test shows 0 failures
No behavioral changes — purely structural refactor
userStories.notes: Keep prop interfaces minimal. Extracted components should receive data via props, not re-fetch.

userStories.title: Extension null-check hardening and Promise error handling
userStories.description: Code review found: (1) popup.js renderSaves() and renderAutoSaveToggle() access DOM elements without null checks, (2) board detection files have unguarded querySelector calls, (3) some service worker message handlers still lack .catch() on Promises. Fix all null-check gaps and add .catch() to every async message handler.
acceptanceCriteria: popup.js: every getElementById and querySelector call is followed by an if-null guard before accessing properties
All board files (content/boards/*.js): every querySelector and querySelectorAll call includes a null/empty check
background.js: every Promise chain in chrome.runtime.onMessage handlers has a .catch() that calls sendResponse with a safe fallback
Extension tests pass: cd extension && npm test shows 0 failures
No functional changes — only defensive null checks and error handling
userStories.notes: Focus on runtime crash prevention. The pattern is: const el = shadow.querySelector(sel); if (!el) return defaultValue;

userStories.title: Fix MSW test configuration to eliminate 64 unhandled request errors
userStories.description: Frontend test suite shows 422 tests passing but 64 'errors' from MSW intercepting unhandled requests. The MSW server config uses onUnhandledRequest: 'error' but many tests trigger requests without matching handlers (e.g., analytics tracking, logo fetches). Add catch-all handlers or passthrough rules for known non-critical endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend test suite runs with 0 errors: npm test shows 'Errors 0' in summary
All 422+ tests still pass
MSW setup in test-utils or setupTests includes passthrough handlers for: POST /api/analytics/*, GET /api/notifications/*, GET /logo.clearbit.com/*, GET /api/applications?stage=Offer*
No test logic changes — only MSW handler additions
Add a comment in the MSW setup explaining which endpoints are passthroughs and why
userStories.notes: Check frontend/src/test-utils.jsx or setupTests.js for the MSW server configuration.

userStories.title: Extract magic numbers to named constants across frontend and backend
userStories.description: Multiple magic numbers found: 86_400_000 (ms/day) in DetailPanel, hardcoded debounce timings, array slice limits in LandingPage, inline pixel offsets. Extract all to named constants in lib/constants.js (frontend) or config.py (backend).
acceptanceCriteria: frontend/src/lib/constants.js exports MS_PER_DAY = 86_400_000
All hardcoded timing values (debounce, dismiss delays) in components use named constants from lib/constants.js
Backend config.py or relevant service files define constants for any hardcoded timeouts or limits
No raw numbers remain in component logic except Tailwind class values, array indices, and 0/1 boolean-like values
All tests pass after the constant extraction
userStories.notes: Don't over-extract. Tailwind classes (mt-2.5, w-10) and simple loop bounds (i=0) are fine as-is.

userStories.title: Extract shared board utility: deduplicate getRemoteStatus across extension boards
userStories.description: The getRemoteStatus() function is copy-pasted across 8+ board detection files in extension/content/boards/. Extract it to a shared utility module (extension/content/boards/board_utils.js) and have each board file import from there.
acceptanceCriteria: New file extension/content/boards/board_utils.js exports getRemoteStatus() and any other shared functions (e.g., extractSalaryRange, normalizeUrl)
board_utils.js is under 100 lines

All board files (linkedin.js, greenhouse.js, lever.js, workday.js, indeed.js, glassdoor.js, wellfound.js, handshake.js, dice.js, remoteok.js, ashby.js) import getRemoteStatus from board_utils.js instead of defining it locally
Extension tests pass: cd extension && npm test shows 0 failures
No behavioral changes — purely structural deduplication
userStories.notes: The extension has no build step, so use ES module imports. Check the manifest.json content_scripts to ensure board_utils.js is included in the script list.

userStories.title: Add missing Python type annotations on all function signatures
userStories.description: Code review found functions missing return type annotations and parameter types in middleware (csrf.py, security_headers.py, cache_control.py dispatch methods) and some service functions. Add type annotations to every function signature in the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Every function in backend/**/*.py has complete type annotations: all parameters typed and return type specified
Middleware dispatch() methods have typed call_next parameter: Callable[[Request], Awaitable[Response]]
Use | union syntax, not Optional (per CLAUDE.md)
pyright or mypy check shows no new type errors (existing ones are acceptable)
All tests pass: pytest tests/ -x shows 0 failures
userStories.notes: Focus on files flagged in the review: middleware/*.py, service files with missing annotations. Don't add annotations to test files.

userStories.title: Real-time notification delivery via Server-Sent Events
userStories.description: Currently notifications are fetched by polling (React Query refetch interval). Add an SSE endpoint GET /api/notifications/stream that pushes new notifications in real-time. The frontend NotificationBell should subscribe to SSE and update the React Query cache on new events.
acceptanceCriteria: New endpoint GET /api/notifications/stream returns text/event-stream with keep-alive every 30s
When a new notification is created (service layer), it is pushed to all active SSE connections for that user_id
Frontend NotificationBell.jsx opens an EventSource connection on mount, closes on unmount
On SSE message, the notification count badge updates immediately without waiting for the next poll
SSE connection auto-reconnects on disconnect (EventSource handles this natively)
Add a test for the SSE endpoint: connect, send a notification, verify it arrives on the stream
Falls back gracefully to polling if SSE connection fails
userStories.notes: Use asyncio.Queue per connection. Store active connections in a module-level dict keyed by user_id. Clean up on disconnect.

userStories.title: Custom application fields: user-defined metadata per application
userStories.description: Users want to track custom data per application (e.g., 'Referral name', 'Team', 'Visa sponsorship'). Add a custom_fields schema (max 10 fields per user, types: text, date, select). Store field definitions in a user_custom_fields collection and field values in application.custom_fields subdocument.
acceptanceCriteria: New collection user_custom_fields with schema: {user_id, fields: [{name, type: 'text'|'date'|'select', options?: string[]}]}
Max 10 custom fields per user enforced at the service layer
PATCH /api/applications/:id accepts custom_fields: {field_name: value} in the body
GET /api/applications returns custom_fields in the response if present
New endpoints: GET /api/custom-fields (list user's field definitions), POST /api/custom-fields (create/update definitions)
Frontend: Settings page gets a 'Custom Fields' section to define fields
Frontend: DetailPanel renders custom fields below the main fields, editable inline
Custom field values appear in CSV export
Tests: at least 3 backend tests (create field, set value on app, export includes custom fields)
userStories.notes: Keep it simple — no complex validation beyond type checking. Select type stores the options list with the field definition.

userStories.title: Document upload: attach resume and cover letter files to applications
userStories.description: Users want to attach their resume and cover letter to each application. Store files as base64 in MongoDB (max 2MB each, PDF/DOCX only). Show upload UI in DetailPanel and allow download.
acceptanceCriteria: ApplicationUpdate schema accepts documents: {resume?: {filename, content_base64, content_type}, cover_letter?: {filename, content_base64, content_type}}
Validation: max 2MB per file (2_097_152 bytes base64), allowed types: application/pdf, application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document
GET /api/applications/:id/documents/:type (type=resume|cover_letter) returns the file with correct Content-Type and Content-Disposition headers
Frontend: DetailPanel shows a Documents section with upload dropzone (drag-and-drop) for resume and cover letter
Upload shows progress indicator and filename after upload
Download button opens file in new tab for PDFs, downloads for DOCX
Existing resume from ResumeSection (plain text) is preserved separately — this is for file attachments
Tests: backend test for upload, download, and size limit rejection; frontend smoke test for upload component
userStories.notes: Base64 in MongoDB is fine for MVP. Future optimization would use GridFS or S3. Max file size is 2MB.

userStories.title: Application deadline tracking with browser push notifications
userStories.description: Add a deadline field to applications. When a deadline is within 48 hours, show a visual indicator on the dashboard. Optionally send a browser push notification (via the service worker) if the user has granted notification permission.
acceptanceCriteria: ApplicationUpdate schema accepts deadline: datetime | None
Dashboard ApplicationRow shows a red clock icon and 'Due in X hours' badge when deadline is within 48 hours
Dashboard StatsBar shows count of upcoming deadlines (next 7 days)
Settings page has a 'Deadline reminders' toggle that requests browser Notification permission
Frontend service worker checks deadlines every hour and shows browser notification for deadlines within 24 hours
DetailPanel shows deadline as an editable date picker field
Tests: backend test for deadline field CRUD; frontend test for deadline badge rendering
userStories.notes: Use the existing sw.js service worker for push notifications. Deadline check runs client-side by comparing cached application data.

userStories.title: Application timeline export and sharing: generate a shareable timeline link
userStories.description: Users want to share their job search journey. Add a 'Share Timeline' feature that generates a read-only public page showing the user's application timeline (stages, dates, outcomes) without revealing sensitive details (salary, notes, contacts).
acceptanceCriteria: New endpoint POST /api/sharing/timeline returns a shareable link with a unique token (reuse existing sharing infrastructure from US-077)
New page /shared/timeline/:token renders a clean, public timeline view
Timeline shows: company names, role titles, stages, dates applied, and outcomes (offer/rejected/withdrawn)
Sensitive fields excluded: salary, notes, contacts, ai_analysis, custom_fields, documents
User can revoke the timeline share from Settings
The shared page has a professional look with the Pipelined branding and a 'Track your own applications' CTA
Tests: backend test for timeline share creation and token validation; frontend smoke test for shared timeline page
userStories.notes: Reuse the sharing/service.py infrastructure from US-077. Add a 'type' field to distinguish pipeline shares from timeline shares.
